differentials.name: Fluid-secreting low-grade primary neoplasm
differentials.raw: Fluid-secreting low-grade primary neoplasm (pilocytic astrocytoma, hemangioblastoma)

differentials.name: hypervascular margin of lesion = granulation tissue / peripheral vascular channels / hypervascular tumor capsule
differentials.raw: hypervascular margin of lesion = granulation tissue / peripheral vascular channels / hypervascular tumor capsule

differentials.name: breakdown of blood-brain barrier = leakage of contrast out of abnormally permeable vessels into extracellular fluid space
differentials.raw: breakdown of blood-brain barrier = leakage of contrast out of abnormally permeable vessels into extracellular fluid space

differentials.name: hypodense center = avascular / hypovascular (requires time to fill) / cystic degeneration
differentials.raw: hypodense center = avascular / hypovascular (requires time to fill) / cystic degeneration

context: Cause: √  wavy undulating rim of > 10 mm in thickness √  enhancing mural nodule within cyst Pathogenesis: Incidence of ring blush: abscess (in 73%); glioblastoma (in 48%); metastasis (in 33%); grade II astrocytoma (in 26%) [NOT in grade I astrocytoma]
